channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: B.C. family seeks government support for gene therapy for son with rare neurodegenerative condition
item.description: A British Columbia family is urgently seeking access to a potential gene therapy for their three-year-old son, Gurmoh, who has been diagnosed with Spastic Paraplegia Type 4, a rare neurodegenerative d...

item.title: The Global Rise of Korean Fried Chicken: A Cultural Phenomenon
item.description: Korean fried chicken, a dish adapted from US soldiers after the Korean War, has become a global sensation as part of the K-food wave. With over 40,000 fried chicken restaurants in South Korea, the dis...

item.title: Kim Chiu and Sofia Andres' Scene in 'The Alibi' Sparks Online Reactions
item.description: The recent episode of ABS-CBN’s series 'The Alibi' has caught the attention of viewers online, especially a scene featuring Kim Chiu and Sofia Andres in a dramatic confrontation. Netizens flooded soci...

item.title: Kerala 2026 Polls: Key Constituencies and Emerging Trends
item.description: The upcoming Kerala election in 2026 is shaping up to be a closely contested battle between the Left Democratic Front (LDF), the United Democratic Front (UDF), and the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). Wh...

item.title: Indian Gangster Rao Inderjeet Yadav Arrested in Dubai After Leading a Lavish Life with Huge Online Following
item.description: Rao Inderjeet Yadav, a notorious Indian gangster with a massive online following of over 10 lakh (1 million) people, has been arrested in Dubai. Yadav had been leading a life of luxury, flaunting his ...

item.title: Retailers face declining sales amid cautious consumer spending
item.description: Australian retailers are experiencing a slowdown as consumers become more cautious with their spending. Rising interest rates and higher grocery bills are squeezing household budgets, leading to a dro...

item.title: Yanga Dominates Pamba Jiji FC to Extend Lead at Top
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) secured a dominant 3-0 victory over Pamba Jiji FC at CCM Kirumba Stadium, extending their lead at the top of the Mainland Premier League standings to nine points. Yanga's perfor...

item.title: Supreme Court affirms judicial review over religious practices subject to constitutional limits
item.description: On Wednesday, the Supreme Court of India firmly reminded the Union government that judicial oversight over religious practices cannot be completely excluded, even when matters of faith are involved. D...

item.title: Actor Michael Campbell, acclaimed for Richard III performance, dies aged 35
item.description: Michael Campbell, also known as Michael Patrick, a celebrated Northern Irish actor, has passed away aged 35 after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease (MND) in February 2023. Campbell, renowned ...

item.title: Getrude Mallya’s Journey in Leading Private Banking in Tanzania
item.description: Getrude Mallya ni kiongozi mwenye uzoefu mkubwa katika sekta ya benki Tanzania, ambaye kwa sasa anasimamia Private Banking katika NMB Bank Plc. Safari yake ilianza kama junior officer katika teller co...

item.title: US Vice President Vance Supports Orbán Amidst Hungarian Election Challenges
item.description: The United States Vice President, JD Vance, is actively supporting Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán as he faces a challenging election campaign. Orbán's political standing has weakened, and Vance...

item.title: Tash Wins Hearts After Helping Stepdaughter Sky Dye Her Hair
item.description: Hiram Gitau, who is popularly known as Tash and was the husband of the late gospel singer Betty Bayo, warmed hearts online after a video surfaced of him helping his stepdaughter, Sky Victor, dye her h...

item.title: Pakistan's Strong Relationship with UAE Remains Intact: Defence Minister Khawaja Asif
item.description: Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif reassured the public that there were no signs of displeasure from the United Arab Emirates (UAE) regarding Pakistan's financial dealings. Asif stated that Paki...

item.title: Yvette Cooper calls for unrestricted reopening of Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions
item.description: In her annual speech on UK foreign policy priorities, Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper is set to urge the full and unconditional reopening of the Strait of Hormuz later on Thursday. This comes after Ir...

item.title: Delhi woman killed by boyfriend in Mangolpuri PG; three arrested
item.description: A 35-year-old woman from Mangolpuri, Delhi, was allegedly murdered by her 22-year-old boyfriend in a paying guest (PG) accommodation, with two other men involved in aiding the crime. The incident came...

item.title: Joe Rogan suggests Trump used Iran conflict to divert attention from Epstein reports
item.description: On a recent episode of his podcast, Joe Rogan discussed the idea that former President Donald Trump might have initiated conflict with Iran to divert public attention from the release of the Epstein f...

item.title: Orlando Pirates close in on new PSL scoring record amid title race
item.description: Orlando Pirates are on the verge of setting a new club scoring record in the Premier Soccer League (PSL) era, following a remarkable series of high-scoring victories. The Buccaneers have netted 45 goa...

item.title: Stephen Colbert Jokes About Trump’s Claims on Iran War Objectives
item.description: On April 9, 2026, Stephen Colbert addressed the recent U.S.-Iran conflict on 'The Late Show,' commenting on former President Donald Trump's statements about the war. Trump claimed on Truth Social that...

item.title: Record US Oil Exports Driven by Iran Conflict and Asian Demand
item.description: United States crude oil exports are poised to reach record levels in April, fuelled by surging demand from Asian customers seeking alternatives to oil supplies disrupted by the ongoing Iran war. Accor...

item.title: Remote Gambling Bill fails to address crucial safeguards for South Africa's online gambling industry
item.description: Dr. Thomas Brennan warns that South Africa’s proposed Remote Gambling Bill could usher in a dangerous era for financial stability if it proceeds without robust consumer protections. Writing during Fin...
